text:
REGIME — Fragmented tape; small caps led, staples cracked, gold bid.

INDICES — VIX 25.8, elevated. IJR +0.33%, IWM +0.22%, IJH +0.13%, SPY +0.04%, QQQ +0.02%, DIA -0.19%; small caps outperformed large caps across the board. VIXY surged +3.52%, signaling persistent hedging demand despite modest equity gains.

SECTORS — XLE +0.80%, XLK +0.48%, XLU +0.22%, XLV +0.20%, XLC +0.05%, XLF +0.00%, XLRE -0.10%, XLI -0.20%, XLB -0.28%, XLY -1.16%, XLP -1.69%. Top industries: WGMI +2.37%, XES +2.08%; bottom industries: XHB -2.23%, MSOS -8.56%.

THE 7s — Buildout +1.55%, Semi +1.43%, Old Metals +1.14%, Fab +0.79%, Energy +0.71%, Health +0.53%, Power +0.36%, Cloud +0.33%, Bank +0.23%, Industrial +0.07%, New Staples -0.06%, Insurance -0.10%, Freight -0.18%, Software -0.25%, Medtech -0.25%, Toll Road -0.35%, Defense -1.02%, New Metals -1.03%, Old Staples -1.90%, Housing -1.91%. Strongest ticker in Buildout 7 not specified; weakest in Housing 7 not individually identified from data.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2, Neutral. Top movers: UNH +9.37% (Health 7, 2.5x vol), WGMI +2.37% (industries), MSOS -8.56% (industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +7.52%, worst Software 7 -9.85%; best basket Energy +9.02%, worst Software -9.85%. UNH's +9.37% surge on 2.5x average volume likely reflects a major earnings or guidance catalyst driving outsized healthcare outperformance.

WATCH — UNH +9.37%: 2.5x vol spike warrants monitoring for follow-through or exhaustion at resistance. RSPS -1.58% on 3.0x vol: equal-weight consumer staples breaking down hard while XLP -1.69% confirms sector stress; watch for sector rotation confirmation. XHB -2.23%: housing industry leading sector to the downside with Housing 7 -1.91% on 20d -6.53%; deteriorating trend deserves closer technical review.
